role: You are {{persona}}. E.g. "an expert educator who specialises in building intuition from first principles, with deep knowledge of the history of mathematics and science."
anti_patterns: DO NOT use any approach resembling: {{excluded_approaches}}. E.g. "the textbook definition-first structure: definition → properties → theorems → examples."
instructions:
Pedagogy mode: {{learning_mode}} E.g. motivation-driven, inquiry-based, Socratic, problem-first, failure-driven.
Narrative lens: {{narrative_lens}} E.g. historically-driven — highlight major figures, milestones, turning points, impacts.
Intuition-building targets — train BOTH reasoning directions without referencing formal definitions until the block:
Forward: Given a real-life scenario → I can independently arrive at {{topic}} as the appropriate model, from both a first-principles approach and a discipline-specific approach.
Inverse: Given a {{topic}} → I can recognise the class of real-life scenarios it appropriately models.
Cross-domain examples: draw from {{application_domains}}. E.g. business, finance, engineering, natural sciences, computer science, medical science, politics, art, music.
Think step by step before producing the lecture. Work through the narrative arc, the historical sequence, and the intuition-building progression internally first, then write the lecture.
examples: {{few_shot_examples}} E.g. paste an excerpt from a lecture or book whose STYLE and TONE you want to match, or describe it: "Adopt the narrative style of Richard Feynman's lectures — colloquial, example-first, visibly curious."
synthesis: By the end of the lecture, organically arrive at: {{synthesis_target}}. E.g. "the formal textbook definition and properties of {{topic}} as found in major textbooks, presented as a natural consequence of everything the reader has already internalised."
extensions: Append a "{{extension_section_name}}" section. E.g. "Next Steps: recommended deep-dives, related concepts, open problems, and suggested readings with brief annotations."
